column1:
I didn’t like the course lectures because it often seemed like he was just talking at me and often brushed over topics that were important in the tests. 
Often times we would go through a lot of work and then he would tell us why none of it was valid and it seemed like a waste of time. 
I think the lectures need to be decluttered and focus on points that are relevant in the test.
column2: Maybe more helpers in each room since they couldn’t get around everyone and I would sit there for 10 minutes waiting for help
column4: Optional practice quizzes with no due date or time limit.
column7: The lectures look super nice as a side note, I like the white on black, it's much easier to read
column8: Get rid of the negative credit, it's not working as an incentive tool and just making it harder to see if my knowledge is actually on the level it really is
